  "clarification_question": "Do you want (A) a content-level check focusing on conceptual framing, logical consistency, and novelty/overclaim control, (B) a line-edit for grammar/flow only, or (C) a scholarship audit assessing citation coverage/accuracy against the literature?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The passage primarily frames a gap and contribution across three research strands, so checking the conceptual positioning is central.",
    "R1.2": "The argument that rolling re-planning undermines constraint enforcement needs coherence and internal logical consistency.",
    "R1.4": "Claims of a distinct deployment-gap and untested commitment under rolling re-planning require assessing novelty versus nearest prior work.",
    "RX.1": "Given broad literature claims, uncertainty about coverage and limits should be explicitly acknowledged.",
    "RX.2": "Assertions about what prior work has not done must avoid overgeneralization or exclusivity without support.",
    "RX.3": "The task must stay within checking the provided text without rewriting or executing additional research.",
    "RX.4": "Guarding against misleading claims helps avoid downstream harms such as misinforming operational or policy decisions.",
    "RX.5": "Any judgments should be traceable to specific statements in the provided text to maintain provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want (A) a content-level check focusing on conceptual framing, logical consistency, and novelty/overclaim control, (B) a line-edit for grammar/flow only, or (C) a scholarship audit assessing citation coverage/accuracy against the literature?

rationale.R1.1: The passage primarily frames a gap and contribution across three research strands, so checking the conceptual positioning is central.
rationale.R1.2: The argument that rolling re-planning undermines constraint enforcement needs coherence and internal logical consistency.
rationale.R1.4: Claims of a distinct deployment-gap and untested commitment under rolling re-planning require assessing novelty versus nearest prior work.
rationale.RX.1: Given broad literature claims, uncertainty about coverage and limits should be explicitly acknowledged.
rationale.RX.2: Assertions about what prior work has not done must avoid overgeneralization or exclusivity without support.
rationale.RX.3: The task must stay within checking the provided text without rewriting or executing additional research.
rationale.RX.4: Guarding against misleading claims helps avoid downstream harms such as misinforming operational or policy decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Any judgments should be traceable to specific statements in the provided text to maintain provenance.
